mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:US unemployment rate was 4.9 percent in January 2016
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion, which compresses input cost margins and lifts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observed windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in new housing starts signals tightening financial conditions and reduced construction demand, which suppresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS stood at 0.34 percent in January 2016
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50 percent of windows where FEDFUNDS rises >0.25pp and HOUST declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth rate versus 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.0 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations become embedded in pricing power, allowing firms to pass costs to consumers and boosting equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with declining unemployment creates a wage-price spiral risk that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy prematurely, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed windows.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions, a divergence where industrial production falls while retail sales remain elevated signals inventory depletion and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where INDPRO drops >1.0 percent MoM while RSAFS does not decline.
